← All Digests Thursday, April 2, 2026

Slack Becomes Enterprise AI's Front Door, Agentic AI Hits Inflection Point, Marketing Goes Modular

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

Salesforce Turns Slack Into the Front End for Enterprise AI

Salesforce is positioning Slackbot as "the ultimate teammate" — an AI that follows you across apps, captures meeting action items, updates CRM records automatically, and routes requests to different enterprise systems. The big shift: instead of AI tools in silos, Slack becomes the single conversational interface for your entire stack. For PMMs, this means less context-switching and more automated follow-through on customer conversations.

Read on MarTech →
2

The Agentic AI Inflection Point: $2T Spending, 6% Deployed

AI spending hits $2.02T globally in 2026, but here's the gap: while 70%+ of companies use generative AI, only 6% have fully deployed agentic systems. The new model generation (GPT-5.4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude 4.6) enables autonomous agents that can plan, reason, and execute without constant supervision. One health system saved 630 hours/week on admin tasks. The "digital workforce" era is real — but adoption is still early.

Read on BuildEZ →
3

Marketing Automation Trends: Platform Modularity Wins

April 2026 trend report: AI predicts user behavior, dynamically personalizes content, and delivers real-time campaign insights. The key insight for startups: modular platforms are beating all-in-one suites. Start with no-code tools like Zapier, layer on AI-driven personalization, and track metrics obsessively. Beauty brands report 94% sales increases from tailored strategies. The era of "calculated investment in AI" has arrived.

Read on Mean CEO →
4

Adobe Summit 2026: "From Tools to AI-Driven Ecosystems"

Adobe Summit (April 20-22, Las Vegas) previews a fundamental shift: moving from tool-based ecosystems to AI-driven ecosystems. This isn't just about adding AI features — it's about rearchitecting how digital commerce and marketing operate. For PMMs watching Adobe's moves, this signals where enterprise marketing platforms are heading: AI as the operating layer, not the add-on.

Read on Viha Digital Commerce →
5

Enterprise Content Automation: The 2026 Stack

Fresh guide on enterprise content automation tools. Key takeaways: Pick Jasper or Writer for high-volume AI content with brand controls (Writer for security, Jasper for collaboration). Use Acrolinx for regulated industries. Choose Seismic for sales-marketing alignment. Most enterprises benefit from combining 2-3 tools rather than seeking all-in-one. The right stack depends on your biggest bottleneck.

Read on TrySight →

💡 My Take

The 6% number matters. Everyone's talking about AI agents, but almost no one has actually deployed them at scale. That gap between "using AI" and "deploying agentic systems" is where the real opportunity lives. Salesforce making Slack the AI interface is smart — they're betting that whoever owns the conversational layer owns the workflow. For PMMs: now's the time to pilot agentic tools before your competitors do.
